title: Daily Briefing — Tuesday, May 19
tldr: Google I/O 2026 went all-in on agents (~100 announcements): Gemini 3.5 Flash, Omni Flash, Antigravity 2.0, Managed Agents in the API, and Spark, a general-purpose personal agent in the Gemini app.
Anthropic answered with infrastructure: self-hosted sandboxes (run agents inside your own VPC; Cloudflare/Daytona/Modal/Vercel on day one) and MCP Tunnels for private tool access.
Creative suites came to Gemini: Adobe, Canva, and CapCut integrations; Blackstone–Google announced a ~$5B TPU joint venture (May 18).

blocks.p: Google shipped Gemini 3.5 Flash (GA, frontier-ish at Flash price), Omni Flash (any-input → any-output, SynthID-watermarked), Antigravity 2.0 (desktop + CLI + SDK), Managed Agents (remote Linux execution in the Gemini API), and Spark, a general personal agent that reasons across your connected apps.
blocks.why: Google is packaging the whole agent stack — model, runtime, and a consumer agent — for its billions of users.
blocks.doIt: If you live in Google's ecosystem, pilot Spark and Managed Agents on one real workflow before defaulting elsewhere.

blocks.p: Anthropic launched self-hosted sandboxes (agents execute inside the customer's VPC; Cloudflare, Daytona, Modal, and Vercel as day-one providers) and a research preview of MCP Tunnels (outbound-only access to private MCP servers).
blocks.why: The enterprise blocker is data residency and control — Anthropic is selling exactly that.
blocks.doIt: For sensitive workloads, evaluate VPC-local agent execution instead of sending data to a vendor cloud.

blocks.p: Adobe (Firefly agent), Canva (Magic Layers), and CapCut are integrating into Gemini; Blackstone and Google announced a ~$5B TPU joint venture (May 18) — more evidence that compute and capital are the real battleground.

blocks.sub: Learn the agent runtime, not just the model
blocks.p: This week's pattern is unmistakable: every lab now ships a runtime (sandboxes, managed agents, tunnels), not just a model. The durable skill is wiring agents to your real tools and data safely — MCP servers, scoped permissions, VPC execution. Pick one workflow and stand up a minimal, permissioned agent end-to-end; those reps will outlast any single model release.

sources: blog.google (I/O 2026) · CNBC · Anthropic · digitalapplied tracker (figures directional where noted).
